news.title: Purdue Engineering to host Latin American academic leaders for inaugural LIDER program
news.summary_en: This May, Purdue Engineering will welcome a select group of engineering academic leaders from across Latin America for the Leadership Institute for Developing Excellence in Research, Education and Administration (LIDER).

news.title: Purdue engineering management students gain global experience through sustainability project in Costa Rica
news.summary_en: Students from Purdue University's Master of Engineering Management (MEM) program are turning classroom knowledge into real-world impact through an international sustainability project in Costa Rica.

news.title: MSE's Bahr joins prestigious ranks of MRS fellows
news.summary_en: Dave Bahr has been selected as a 2026 Materials Research Society (MRS) fellow in recognition of his sustained and distinguished contributions to the advancement of materials research.

news.title: The Purdue 1 challenge for AAE's Collicott: Don't look out the window
news.summary_en: Purdue professor's research responsibilities will leave little time for sightseeing during the historic spaceflight

news.title: Purdue Engineering student entrepreneurs receive $50,000 from Purdue Innovates Incubator
news.summary_en: Purdue Engineering student entrepreneurs won thousands of dollars from the Purdue Innovates Incubator during the finals of the 2026 New Venture Challenge.

news.title: Motorsports Engineering Club in Indianapolis places silver at 2026 Purdue Grand Prix
news.summary_en: Thirty-three drivers and teams competed in “The Greatest Spectacle in College Racing.” Rounding out the winners’ podium were Sam Griffin, driving for Motorsports Engineering Club in Indianapolis — CED Industrial Kart 4, and Ryan Fiorentino, driving for Tark Racing 29.

news.title: ME's Cappelleri designs robot to move tiny clumps of cells
news.summary_en: In tissue engineering, the tiniest bit of improper force can harm a living culture. 3D clumps of cells - known as spheroids - can be used to model complex human tissues, because they can re-create specific cell-to-cell and cell-to-matrix interactions.
